test: Quantitative D-dimer immunoassay (turbidimetric / ELISA / point-of-care) interpreted at a fixed 500 ng/mL FEU cutoff, an age-adjusted cutoff (10 x age in years for >=50yo), or a YEARS / PEGED conditional / hierarchical cutoff
reference_standard: CT pulmonary angiogram (CTPA) at presentation in patients with positive D-dimer / pre-test probability above the diagnostic-pathway threshold, OR symptomatic VTE confirmed at 3-month clinical follow-up in patients managed without imaging - composite reference
target_condition: Pulmonary embolism (PE); some studies include any venous thromboembolism (VTE) as the 3-month outcome
population: Emergency-department or outpatient adults with clinically suspected PE; usually stratified by Wells score (or simplified Wells / Geneva) into low / moderate / unlikely strata; high-pretest-probability patients are excluded by design - they bypass D-dimer and go directly to CTPA

primary_tier.raw_quote: 3,465 patients with clinically suspected pulmonary embolism. Pulmonary embolism was diagnosed in 456 (13%) of 3,465 patients at baseline. CT pulmonary angiography was not indicated in 1,651 (48%) patients based on the YEARS algorithm, compared with 1,174 (34%) using the Wells rule and fixed D-dimer threshold (absolute difference 14%, 95% CI 12-16; p<0.0001). The 3-month incidence of symptomatic venous thromboembolism after the exclusion of pulmonary embolism by the YEARS algorithm without CT pulmonary angiography was 0.43% (95% CI 0.17-0.88).

Among 3,346 patients with non-high pre-test probability of pulmonary embolism, 19% had pulmonary embolism. The proportion of patients in whom pulmonary embolism could be excluded based on a D-dimer below the age-adjusted cutoff (in patients aged >=50 years, age x 10 microg/L) without diagnostic imaging was 30% (200/673) compared with 6.4% using the conventional 500-microg/L cutoff. Of 331 patients with a D-dimer between 500 microg/L and the age-adjusted cutoff who were not anticoagulated, the 3-month thromboembolic event rate was 0.3% (95% CI 0.1-1.7%).

Among 2,017 patients with clinically suspected pulmonary embolism, the prevalence of pulmonary embolism was 7.4%. With the use of a strategy of a D-dimer level of less than 1000 ng per millilitre with a low clinical pretest probability and a level of less than 500 ng per millilitre with a moderate clinical pretest probability to exclude pulmonary embolism, 1,325 patients (66%) had pulmonary embolism ruled out without imaging. Among these 1,325 patients, none had venous thromboembolism during the 3-month follow-up period (95% CI 0.00 to 0.29%).

primary_tier.pmid_status: unverified - prior PMID 29669607 was a Medicaid/CABG paper; prior DOI 10.1016/S2352-3026(18)30048-6 was a haemophilia paper; the cited van der Pol Lancet Haematol 2019 ADJUST/age-adjusted-validation paper could not be re-located in PubMed during the 2026-04-28 verification round. Treat as placeholder until re-verified against the source full-text.
primary_tier.raw_quote: 1,700 consecutive outpatients suspected of pulmonary embolism. Pulmonary embolism was diagnosed in 272 (16%). Use of the age-adjusted D-dimer cutoff in those with non-high clinical probability allowed exclusion of pulmonary embolism in 660 (46%) of 1,428 non-high probability patients. The 3-month venous thromboembolism rate in patients with normal D-dimer below age-adjusted threshold was 0.7% (95% CI 0.0-1.7%) at follow-up.

3,094 consecutive patients with clinically suspected acute pulmonary embolism. Pulmonary embolism was classified as 'unlikely' (Wells score <=4) and D-dimer was normal in 1,028 (32.6%) patients; the 3-month venous thromboembolism rate in this group was 0.5% (95% CI 0.2-1.1). The overall prevalence of pulmonary embolism at presentation was 20.4% (n=653). Sens of fixed 500 ng/mL cutoff in the Wells-unlikely subset = 98.5%; Spec = 41.4%.

sensitivity_tier_added.raw_quote: 582 patients with clinically suspected pulmonary embolism in the primary-care + ED outpatient pathway. Wells score plus fixed 500 ng/mL D-dimer cutoff. PE was diagnosed in 80 (13.7%) at baseline / 3-month VTE follow-up. Sensitivity of Wells-low + D-dimer-negative strategy: 98.8%; Specificity: 41.2%; 3-month VTE rate among PE-excluded: 0.4% (95% CI 0.0-1.4%).

_engine_expected_flags.note: Documentation only - the engine recomputes at page-load
_engine_expected_flags.expected_fallback_combined: REML or FE bivariate (k=6 - REML may converge or fall back depending on heterogeneity)
_engine_expected_flags.expected_fallback_primary: REML or FE bivariate (k=5 - boundary case)
heterogeneity_axes: cutoff_strategy (fixed_500 vs age_adjusted vs years_conditional vs peged)
reference_standard_specific (all CTPA_plus_3month in current dataset - homogeneous)
_engine_expected_flags.structural_note: D-dimer is a rule-out test by design: Sens approaches 100% (FN approaches 0) in management-outcome studies because the 3-month-VTE endpoint plus the management decision to anticoagulate imaging-positive patients suppresses the FN count. Spec is therefore the dominant discriminator across cutoff strategies.
